chief_complaint: A patient experienced acute coronary syndrome following a stent fracture implanted 3 years previously.
history: The patient had a coronary stent implanted 3 years prior to the current presentation. Risk factors for stent fracture include vessel tortuosity, hinge motion, overlapping stents, increased stent length, smaller stent diameter, and high post-dilation pressure.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are provided.
labs: No specific laboratory or imaging results are provided, but the patient's presentation was consistent with acute coronary syndrome, likely with ECG changes and elevated cardiac biomarkers.
correct_diagnosis: ST-elevation myocardial infarction caused by stent fracture
differentials: ST-elevation myocardial infarction caused by stent fracture
correct_ranking: ST-elevation myocardial infarction caused by stent fracture
